intro: Corporate attorney Zack Shapiro reported that AI automation has eliminated routine legal work, allowing him to focus on higher-value tasks. Citi CEO Jane Fraser warned that banks face dual imperatives to monetize AI while safeguarding financial systems, acknowledging workforce displacement is already occurring. Separately, China's regulators enforced new rules requiring Doubao and Qwen to remove user-created AI agents, marking the first major framework governing humanlike AI interaction.

whitelist.title: AI got this lawyer away from his desk: 'Dramatically more efficient'
whitelist.desc: Corporate attorney Zack Shapiro told Business Insider that, thanks to AI, he has "automated away" all of the grunt work he used to do.
other_outlets: Chiang Rai Times (CTN NEWS) - Chiang Rai News

longtail.desc: The AI investment cycle is most likely to end not because US hyperscalers cut spending, but because markets start pushing back against the lack of returns, Global Brokerage firm Jefferies said in a report. The report argued the turning point will come when investors focus on what it calls a massive wealth transfer from hyperscalers' balance sheets to North Asia. longtail.title: Alibaba to ban employees using Claude Code from July 10 as US-China AI rivalry escalates
longtail.desc: Alibaba is expected to prohibit all its employees from using Claude Code, Anthropic’s popular AI programming tool, from July 10 onwards. The Chinese tech giant has classified Claude Code as a high-risk software. Instead, Alibaba staffers have been instructed to use the company’s own AI coding tool, Qoder, according to multiple news reports.
